Check SubComm profile migration status and force calendar resync

next_best_action: Contact crew member to gather specifics: which subsystems are inaccessible, what anomaly readouts appear, what console they are using, and when the issue started. Cannot triage effectively without more detail.
remediation_steps: Contact crew member to gather specific details about the issue
Determine which subsystems and applications are affected
Check if the issue is isolated to this crew member or affecting others in Stellar Cartography
Once root cause is identified, route to the appropriate specialist team

next_best_action: Close — this is an automated out-of-station reply to a maintenance notification, not a mission signal. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — auto-reply detected, close signal

next_best_action: Initiate security incident investigation. Review authentication logs for unauthorized access, check personal data vault audit trail for document access from the suspicious relay, and verify that the passphrase change and biometric verification are in effect.

Check data vault audit trail for any unauthorized file access or downloads
Verify passphrase change was successful and biometric verification is active

Brief crew member on interim security measures and holographic display crew account for further anomalies

Check access control role assignments on the Deep Storage Array in rg-telemetry-prod

Investigate why the role assignment was removed during the station security audit to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate production crew database connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a crew-facing production incident — 40% of data requests are failing regardless of the submitter’s priority classification.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary data node
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary data node as immediate mitigation
Verify backup data node health and confirm it is handling redirected traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on connection pool exhaustion

Verify Deck 7 access rights were properly migrated from the old system

Escalate to station facilities management if airlock access code and permissions are both confirmed working
Follow up with crew member to confirm resolution

Determine scope of external access, identify who created the sharing link, and assess notification obligations.

Pull mission log audit logs to identify who created the public sharing link and when

Document findings for the incident report and regulatory record

next_best_action: Investigate 200ms-to-50ms latency regression on the APAC sector probe deployment path before the Europa shift. Check comms path to the relay station, recent defense grid or routing changes, and whether the latency is on CDSS’s side or the relay station side.

Review recent defense grid rule, routing, or relay proxy changes that may affect the deployment path
Compare current latency baseline against historical data to confirm the regression

Reissue the FlightOS unified crew authentication logon ticket or have the crew member authenticateto FlightOS with explicit biometric credentials as a workaround
If unified crew authentication token regeneration doesn’t resolve it, check whether the passphrase policy change affected the FlightOS SNC configuration
Confirm FlightOS access is restored and the crew member can complete month-end resource reconciliation

next_best_action: Investigate molecular fabricator queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew members affected and jobs are queuing but not fabricating, which suggests a network or fabricator server issue rather than a console problem.
remediation_steps: Identify the fabricator model and check whether it is network-attached or connected via fabricator server
Check fabricator server spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify network connectivity between the fabricator and the fabricator server or direct network path
If network-connected, check the fabricator’s IP address and network interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh job

next_best_action: Redirect to Station Facilities — briefing room booking and catering are not Station Operations functions. Provide brief guidance on how to book rooms via SubComm (add room resource) and direct catering questions to Station Facilities.
remediation_steps: Reply with instructions: in SubComm, create a new meeting and click ‘Add a room’ to search for available briefing rooms
Note that catering requests should go to the Station Facilities team, not Station Operations
If the crew member cannot see room resources in SubComm, verify their crew account has the room finder permission and route to Mission Software Operations if needed

next_best_action: Renew or replace the quantum encryption certificate on crew-api.cdss.space before it expires on March 20. This is a production-facing API gateway — expiry will break all external fleet integrations. Treat as P1 given 3-day window and the fact that the original provisioner transferred off-station.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or Let’s Encrypt)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a CSR, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the API gateway
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all fleet connections work without TLS errors
Configure auto-renewal and holographic displaying alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate workstation console performance degradation. Symptoms (slow performance, fans running, pop-ups about ‘updating system components’) are more consistent with a stuck ShipOS update or background ShipOS MDM policy compliance than malicious code, especially since SentinelGrid found nothing. Check update history and system resource usage.
remediation_steps: Check ShipOS update history — verify whether recent updates completed or are stuck retrying
Review Task Manager for high CPU/disk processes (likely ShipOS Update or ShipOS MDM compliance)
If updates are stuck, run ShipOS Update troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify SentinelGrid definitions are current and run a quick scan to confirm no threats
If the crew member opened a suspicious subspace message, forward the message to threats@cdss.space and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a hostile signal/scam transmission and advise the crew member NOT to click any links. Forward the original transmission to threats@cdss.space for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn’t clicked.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a scam transmission — do NOT click any links or provide any personal information
Have the crew member forward the original transmission as an attachment to threats@cdss.space
Check whether the transmission bypassed CDSS’s comm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already clicked the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate Station Core VM prod-analytics-01 stuck at ‘Applying station settings’ after scheduled OS patching reboot. Command analytics dashboard dependent on this VM — 2-hour window before the 9 AM briefing.
remediation_steps: Check Station Core boot diagnostics serial console output for the stuck state
Attempt to connect via Station Core Serial Console to bypass the stuck access attempt screen
If Serial Console access is possible, check if a policy or startup script is hanging
If the VM is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the analytics dashboard is accessible once the VM is restored
Post-incident: review the OS patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
